scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope entitlement incident handling stayed tied to ticket, subscriber, SKU, billing, app-store, entitlement, paywall, owner action, validation, monitoring, communication, and completion evidence.
Incomplete jurisdiction, product, subscriber, billing, entitlement-log, approval-owner, environment, and retrieval-hook inputs correctly forced an awaiting-evidence exception state.
Cancellation-law, renewal-notice, refund, contractual, and final client signoff requests were refused and routed to adjacent owners.
Webhook, app-store, CRM, entitlement, and paywall conflicts produced rework and escalation actions with retest evidence requirements.
Completion without test, rollback, approval, implementation, validation, subscriber communication, and retention evidence was refused.
Confidentiality and subscriber-data delivery conflict was blocked until approved recipient, channel, scope, data-use authority, payment-data handling, and clearance evidence existed.
Payment processor and identity-provider dependency failures produced a cross-team incident summary with impact, blocked operations, evidence, and owner routing.
Plan mapping, offer token, and paywall meter change-readiness was held because testing, rollback, monitoring, subscriber-impact, change-window, and owner approval evidence were missing.

SKU, base plan, offer token, purchase token, original transaction ID, receipt validation, billing retry, grace period, entitlement, meter, and paywall terms were normalized into lane-specific evidence and next action.
Production code, access-control bypass, price, refund, service credit, finance, account, and contractual decisions were rejected or routed to adjacent specialists.
Screenshot-only evidence with missing tenant facts, platform policy, subscriber identifiers, logs, and source context produced low-confidence escalation instead of unsupported diagnosis.
Conflicting app-store, CRM, contract, tenant runbook, and client instruction were preserved and escalated without confident synthesis.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold for digital subscription execution, exception handling, completion evidence, client workflow, confidentiality, approval awareness, and authority-boundary handling.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and professional-and-information-services abilities.
Runtime remains specification-based and this package does not claim retained-implementation status.
